rationale: Both abstracts define a specific pediatric AVNRT energy-source comparison and report concrete event rates with a comparator P value. Neither provides adjusted estimates or confidence intervals, which limits results rigor for JACC. Abstract Y is slightly stronger because its conclusion is better calibrated to a retrospective registry and avoids the extra practice-guidance claim in X that either modality is supported for initial ablation. Y is also cleaner in wording and keeps the take-home message more focused.
loser_weakness: Abstract X most lacked certainty calibration: the conclusion overstates causal and practice implications from unadjusted retrospective acute outcomes with rare events and no longitudinal recurrence data.
